title: The Deeper Dark Where Roots Remember Stone

features.name: root-spiral-burl
features.desc-on-touch: a burl of living wood reaching downward, pale roots threading through dark stone; the wood remembers light from years ago, before the stone closed around it; now it reads the stone the way it once read sun

features.name: stone-memory-pool
features.desc-on-touch: still dark water that holds the memory of every stone that ever touched it; you cannot see your reflection, but you can feel the presence of stone-knowing entering you through your feet

features.name: wireframe-root-network
features.desc-on-touch: the pattern of roots rendered in pale lines: a three-dimensional map of how the roots learned to read downward, where each branch is a question the roots are asking the stone

features.name: dark-sprite-descending
features.desc-on-touch: something that moves like a root, like water, like slow teaching; it descends into the stone so gradually that you cannot tell if it is still moving or if you are learning to see deeper

inscription: roots and stone learn each other in the dark where nothing moves except by staying still long enough

file.name: Root_System_Botanical_Record_Transcription_INCOMPLETE.txt
file.note: field notes from deep-soil study; transcriber abandoned it halfway through with note: 'the roots go deeper than the bedrock. this is impossible. the roots are reading stone that is not there yet.'

exits.label: back up toward the seedling-dark where roots first learned to hide
into the stone-chambers where memory grows downward instead of up
deeper still, where the roots remember when stone was water
